scenario.context: Station 07: inspect infrastructure and network components, and document results.
scenario.mission: You derive a testing and documentation approach for acceptance from target values.

constraints: Define target values clearly before measuring
Do not only find deviations, classify them
Think about acceptance and operations documentation together

customerRequest.subject: Station 07: Testing and documentation
customerRequest.body: Please create a solid approach for infrastructure tests, measurements, and documentation.

questions.title: Define testing scope
questions.prompt: Which tests should at least be planned before acceptance? (Multiple choice)

options.text: Availability of central services in all relevant segments
options.rationale: Tests the core function of the target state.

options.text: Test segment boundaries and access rules
options.rationale: Secures protection and correct network separation.
That does not replace a structured test.

options.text: Compare measurements against defined target values
Collect errors only informally in chat
options.rationale: That is too informal for acceptance and follow-up work.

feedback.correct: Correct: The testing scope is suitable for a solid acceptance process.
feedback.wrong: Partially wrong: Please secure function, protection, and target-value reference more strongly.

questions.title: Calculate test pass rate
questions.prompt: Of 48 planned test cases, 44 passed successfully. What is the pass rate in percent, rounded?

feedback.wrong: Please relate passed and planned test cases correctly.

questions.title: Handle deviations properly
questions.prompt: What is the most sensible response to a measured deviation above the target range?

options.text: Ignore the deviation if the rest looks fine
options.rationale: That would be risky for acceptance.

options.text: Only note it verbally and move it to the next appointment
options.rationale: That makes follow-up and approval uncertain.

options.text: Classify the deviation, document it, and trigger a retest
options.rationale: This keeps quality assurance traceable and controllable.

feedback.correct: Correct: Deviations must be handled systematically.
feedback.wrong: Not suitable: A deviation needs classification, documentation, and retesting.

questions.title: From testing to acceptance
questions.prompt: Put the flow from test to final documentation in order.

feedback.correct: Correct: The sequence leads logically to acceptance.
feedback.wrong: Not ideal: Please first test, then evaluate, retest, and document.

questions.title: Formulate a measurement report
questions.prompt: Describe in 4-6 sentences what a good measurement and acceptance report for this project should look like.
questions.expectedOutline: Report with target-value reference, deviation classification, and clear remaining items.

feedback.correct: Correct: The report is described in a way that supports acceptance and operations.
feedback.wrong: Please make the target-value reference, deviations, and remaining items more visible.
